semantic.role: Prompt context diagnostics module that produces structured metadata about which rule blocks and dependency candidates were retained or dropped during context assembly. Provides transparent introspection into trimming and selection decisions.
responsibilities: Build a diagnostic report of retained and dropped rule blocks by comparing raw and final rule data using rule-ID extraction and block splitting.
Build a diagnostic report of dependency selection decisions by mapping context resolution diagnostics against the final rendered context data.
Serve as a stable entry point for external modules (e.g., prompt builder, context engine) to obtain diagnostic snapshots of the context assembly process.
outOfScope: Actual trimming or selection logic (delegated to trim.ts and context engine).
Logging, rendering, or persistence of diagnostics.
Rule block splitting algorithm itself (imported from trim.js).
invariants: The module must remain pure and side-effect-free; it only transforms input strings and diagnostics data into structured diagnostic output.
Diagnostic structures must faithfully reflect the actual retained/dropped blocks and candidates as determined by the raw vs final data comparison.
Rule ID extraction must match the canonical '[Rule: ...]' format used by the rule loading pipeline.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide a diagnostic layer that exposes the decision-making of context assembly, enabling agents and developers to audit why specific rules or dependencies were included or excluded in the prompt context.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Part of the ArchSpine v1.0.0 governance release, introducing diagnostic introspection into the semantic context assembly pipeline to improve transparency and debuggability.

publicSurface.description: Compares raw and final rule block data to produce a diagnostic object listing which rule blocks were retained and which were dropped, each tagged with their rule ID.

Takes context resolution diagnostics (retained dependency candidates) and the final context string, then produces a diagnostic object mapping which candidates were included or excluded in the final context.

English.role: A diagnostics module within the prompt-context subsystem. It builds structured reports that show which rule blocks and dependency candidates survived trimming and selection during context assembly, offering transparency into how the prompt context is composed.
responsibilities: Compare the raw rule data before trimming against the final rule data after trimming to identify which rule blocks (by ID) were retained and which were dropped.
Cross-reference the dependency candidates that were originally retained during context resolution with the final rendered context string to determine which ones were actually included.
Expose these diagnostics via two pure functions so that calling code (e.g., the prompt builder or context engine) can attach diagnostic metadata to the assembled context.
outOfScope: The actual trimming logic (splitRuleBlocks comes from trim.js).
Any output formatting, logging, or writing of diagnostics to disk or console.
Logic for context resolution or relevance scoring of dependencies (handled by context.ts).

exports.name: buildDependencySelectionDiagnostics
exports.signature:
buildDependencySelectionDiagnostics(
  dependencyDiagnostics: ContextResolutionDiagnostics | undefined,
  finalContextData: string,
)
